current_markdown:
# Requirement Brief

## Goal

The goal of this change is to enhance the loan calculator so that, when a customer enters the loan duration, the calculator can determine and display the corresponding loan amount, monthly payment, and interest rate. This should provide customers with a clearer and more useful self-service experience when exploring loan options.

## Business Context

This requirement is driven by a quality-of-life improvement for customers. The business need is to make the loan calculator more helpful and intuitive by allowing customers to understand key loan parameters from the loan duration input without requiring additional manual effort or assistance.

By improving the calculator experience, the bank can support better customer engagement and make the loan journey easier to understand. This may also reduce friction during product exploration and improve the overall digital experience for end users.

## Users

The primary users are customers using the loan calculator in a digital banking context. These users are expected to input the loan duration and review the resulting loan-related outputs to support their borrowing decisions.

## Functional Requirements

The loan calculator must accept loan duration as an input and use that input to calculate and display the loan amount, monthly payment, and interest rate. The system should present these outputs in a clear and understandable format for customers.

The calculator should respond correctly when the loan duration is entered or updated, ensuring the displayed results reflect the latest input. The calculation logic should support the expected customer journey of comparing loan scenarios based on duration.

## Non-Functional Requirements

The solution must meet security and privacy expectations appropriate for a banking application. Customer input and any derived financial information must be handled securely and in accordance with applicable privacy requirements.

The feature should also be implemented in a way that preserves trust and reliability in the calculator experience. Results should be presented consistently and without exposing unnecessary personal or sensitive data.

## Risks and Dependencies

No specific risks or external dependencies have been identified at this time. If calculation logic depends on existing pricing rules, product configuration, or interest rate models, those dependencies should be confirmed during implementation planning.

## Open Questions

None at the moment.
